contents.value:
```makefile
build: main.o utils.o
	gcc -o app main.o utils.o
```
**Targets** define what to build. Prerequisites are checked first; if any are newer than the target, the recipe runs.

```makefile
.PHONY: all build test clean
```
**.PHONY** declares targets that aren't files. Without it, `make clean` won't run if a file named `clean` exists.

```makefile
CC := gcc          # simple expansion
CFLAGS = -Wall     # recursive expansion
PORT ?= 8080       # conditional (default)
LDFLAGS += -lm     # append
```
**Variables** store values for reuse. `:=` expands immediately, `=` expands on use, `?=` only if unset.

```makefile
%.o: %.c
	$(CC) -c $< -o $@

# $@  = target name
# $<  = first prerequisite
# $^  = all prerequisites
# $*  = stem (matched %)
```
**Automatic variables** are set by Make for each rule. They refer to the target and prerequisites.

```makefile
GIT_HASH := $(shell git rev-parse --short HEAD)
FILES := $(shell find src -name '*.go')
```
**$(shell ...)** executes a command and captures stdout. Used to get dynamic values like git hashes or file lists.

```makefile
# CORRECT (tab):
build:
	go build ./...

# WRONG (spaces):
build:
    go build ./...
```
**Recipe lines MUST use TAB** characters, not spaces. This is the #1 Makefile gotcha. Configure your editor to insert real tabs.

```makefile
include Makefile.common
-include .env.mk   # - means don't error if missing
```
**include** reads another Makefile. Prefix with `-` to silently ignore if the file doesn't exist.

```makefile
ifeq ($(OS),Windows_NT)
  BINARY := app.exe
else
  BINARY := app
endif
```
**Conditionals** (`ifeq`, `ifneq`, `ifdef`, `ifndef`) control which parts of the Makefile are processed.

```makefile
%.o: %.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@
```
**Pattern rules** use `%` as a wildcard. This rule says: to make any `.o` file, compile the corresponding `.c` file.
**Prerequisites** are files that must exist before building a target.
A **recipe** is a shell command (tab-indented) that builds the target.

**Automatic variables** refer to target/prereqs.
```
$@ = target, $< = first prereq, $^ = all prereqs
```

**wildcard** function finds files matching a pattern.
```makefile
SRCS = $(wildcard src/*.c)
```

**Functions** transform text.
```makefile
$(patsubst %.c,%.o,$(SRCS))
$(filter %.c,$(FILES))
```

**$(shell ...)** executes a shell command and captures output.
```makefile
DATE = $(shell date +%Y-%m-%d)
```

**vpath** specifies search directories for prerequisites.
```makefile
vpath %.c src
vpath %.h include
```

**define** creates a multi-line variable.
```makefile
define COMPILE_TEMPLATE
$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@
endef
```

**export** makes a variable available to sub-makes and recipes.
```makefile
export PATH := $(HOME)/bin:$(PATH)
```

```makefile
VAR := value
```
Simple expansion variable. Value is expanded at the time of assignment.

```makefile
VAR = value
```
Recursive expansion variable. Value is expanded each time the variable is referenced.

```makefile
VAR ?= value
```
Conditional assignment. Only sets the variable if it is not already defined.

```makefile
VAR += value
```
Append to an existing variable's value.

```makefile
$@
```
Automatic variable: the file name of the target of the rule.

```makefile
$<
```
Automatic variable: the name of the first prerequisite.

```makefile
$^
```
Automatic variable: the names of all prerequisites, with spaces between them.

```makefile
$*
```
Automatic variable: the stem with which an implicit rule matches.

```makefile
$(patsubst pattern,replacement,text)
```
Pattern substitution - replace words matching pattern with replacement.

```makefile
ifeq (a,b) ... endif
```
Conditional directive - evaluate the enclosed text only if the two arguments are equal.

```makefile
\t(TAB character)
```
Recipe lines MUST start with a TAB character, not spaces. This is a common source of errors.

```makefile
@command
```
Prefix a recipe line with @ to suppress echoing the command itself.

```makefile
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```makefile
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```makefile
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```makefile
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.
